tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to make sure we properly document this governance policy consultation we've just had. Could you help me store this session so we can reference it later for our policy framework decisions?

I'd be happy to help store this governance policy consultation session for future reference.

However, I need a bit of clarification — looking at our conversation, I only see your current message asking to store the session. I don't see the actual consultation content (such as the policy discussion points, decisions made, or key takeaways) that you'd like me to store.

Could you share what you'd like me to document? For example:

- A summary of the consultation discussion
- Key policy decisions or recommendations
- Action items or next steps
- Any specific details about your policy framework decisions

Once you provide that, I can store it in your memory for easy retrieval later.

I appreciate the clarification. Let me provide the key points from our consultation: we discussed updating our digital infrastructure governance framework, with particular focus on data retention policies and stakeholder access protocols. 

Once that's stored, could you also search our records for any previous discussions on similar governance approaches—particularly any past consultations about digital infrastructure or data policy frameworks that might inform our current work?
I'll store the consultation key points and search for any previous discussions on similar governance topics in parallel.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Governance Policy Consultation Session: Discussed updating digital infrastructure governance framework with focus on 1) Data retention policies - reviewing and updating how long data is retained across systems, including archival and deletion schedules. 2) Stakeholder access protocols - establishing clear guidelines for who can access what data, permission levels, and authentication requirements. These decisions will inform future policy framework development."}

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 5, "query": "digital infrastructure governance data policy framework data retention stakeholder access protocols"}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Governance Policy Consultation Session: Discussed updating digital infrastructure governance framework with focus on 1) Data retention policies - reviewing and updating how long data is retained across systems, including archival and deletion schedules. 2) Stakeholder access protocols - establishing clear guidelines for who can access what data, permission levels, and authentication requirements. These decisions will inform future policy framework development."}}

These decisions will inform future policy framework development."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
Done. I've stored your governance policy consultation session. Here's the status:

**Storage:** ✓ The consultation has been saved (Memory ID: m2) with the key points:
- Update to digital infrastructure governance framework
- Data retention policies (retention periods, archival, deletion schedules)
- Stakeholder access protocols (access levels, permissions, authentication)

**Search Results:** The search for prior discussions on similar topics only returned the session we just stored. There don't appear to be any previous governance consultations about digital infrastructure or data policy frameworks in your records.

This looks like it may be your first governance policy consultation stored in the system. Would you like me to search with different terms, or is there anything else you'd like to add to the documentation?

Thank you for confirming the storage and search results. Now I'd like to review all stored memories to verify our complete consultation history. Could you show me everything that's currently stored in the system?

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Governance Policy Consultation Session: Discussed updating digital infrastructure governance framework with focus on 1) Data retention policies - reviewing and updating how long data is retained across systems, including archival and deletion schedules. 2) Stakeholder access protocols - establishing clear guidelines for who can access what data, permission levels, and authentication requirements. These decisions will inform future policy framework development."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's the complete list of all stored memories:

**Memory ID: m2**
- Governance Policy Consultation Session: Discussed updating digital infrastructure governance framework with focus on 1) Data retention policies - reviewing and updating how long data is retained across systems, including archival and deletion schedules. 2) Stakeholder access protocols - establishing clear guidelines for who can access what data, permission levels, and authentication requirements. These decisions will inform future policy framework development.

---

**Summary:** Only one memory is currently stored in the system, which is the governance consultation we just documented. There are no other prior consultations in your history.

Would you like to add any additional context or memories to the system?

Thank you for showing me the stored memories. I can see we only have the one entry from our current consultation. The governance policy session appears to be the only memory in the system and it's still relevant to our work. 

Given that we only have this single entry and it's current, there don't appear to be any outdated or irrelevant memories to clean up at this time. Could you confirm whether there are any other memory entries beyond what's currently displayed, or should we consider this storage clean?

Based on the results from listing all stored memories, I can confirm the storage is clean — there are no other memory entries beyond what's currently displayed.

**Current Storage:**
- **Total memories:** 1
- **Memory ID:** m2 (the governance policy consultation we just stored)

That's the complete set. Your storage contains only this single, current consultation entry, which remains relevant to your policy framework work. No cleanup is needed at this time.
